input.title: Grocery Delivery Application
input.description: Grocery Delivery Application is a web-based enterprise platform that centralizes operational workflows, data visibility, and cross-functional reporting.

user_classes.Admin: Configures the system, manages users, and oversees permissions and settings.
user_classes.Manager: Oversees departmental workflows, approvals, KPIs, and operational reporting.
user_classes.Employee: Performs day-to-day operational tasks within assigned modules and permissions.
user_classes.User: Generic authenticated platform user with access limited by role and department.

definitions.Admin: System user with full administrative privileges.
definitions.Manager: Operational owner of a department, module, or workflow.
definitions.Employee: End user performing role-specific business operations.

constraints: Initial release will focus on the web platform only
Role-based access control is mandatory across all modules
Operational data must be auditable and retained according to policy

operating_environment.frontend: Web-based interface built with React Native and accessible through modern browsers.
operating_environment.backend: Service layer implemented with Node.js, MongoDB, Redis for business operations.
operating_environment.deployment: Cloud-hosted environment with centralized monitoring, backups, and automated deployments.

NFR-01.title: Security and Access Control
requirements: All sensitive data must be encrypted in transit and at rest
Role-based authorization must restrict access by module and action
All authentication events and privileged actions must be logged

NFR-02.title: Performance and Scalability
requirements: Core pages and dashboards should load within acceptable enterprise SLAs
The platform must support concurrent usage across multiple departments
Background jobs and reporting workloads should not block transactional workflows

NFR-03.title: Reliability and Recoverability
requirements: Automated backups must run on a defined schedule
Critical workflows should support retry-safe processing and audit logging
Operational alerts should be generated for failures and degraded services

system_attributes.reliability: Platform should provide resilient transactional processing with backup and recovery capabilities.
system_attributes.scalability: Architecture should support horizontal scaling for APIs, background workers, and reporting services.
system_attributes.security: Authentication, authorization, encryption, and auditability must be built into every module.
system_attributes.maintainability: Codebase should remain modular, observable, and safe to extend through clear service boundaries.

out_of_scope: Native mobile applications in the first release
Large third-party marketplace or legacy ERP migrations unless explicitly scoped
Non-core experimental modules outside the platform's operational workflows

FR-01.title: User Registration
requirements: Register with phone number or email
Set delivery address with map pin

FR-02.title: Store Browsing
requirements: Display nearby grocery stores based on location
Store details with hours, delivery time, and minimum order

FR-03.title: Product Catalog
requirements: Browse products by category and subcategory
Product details with nutritional info and alternatives

FR-04.title: Smart Shopping Lists
requirements: Add items from browsing or manually

FR-05.title: Shopping Cart
requirements: Cart total with delivery fee and taxes

FR-09.title: Shopper App
requirements: View assigned orders with item lists
Mark order as ready for delivery

FR-10.title: Rating and Tips
requirements: Refund request for missing or damaged items

FR-01.title: Design User Registration UI
FR-01.description: Design the user interface for user registration. Create the screens, forms, dashboards, or status views needed by the SRS and make the workflow clear, responsive, and accessible for the target users.
acceptance_criteria: UI design for user registration is complete and reviewable.
The user registration design is responsive and accessible.
The design for user registration aligns with the documented user flow and validation states.

FR-01.title: Frontend Implementation of User Registration
FR-01.description: Implement the frontend behavior for user registration. Build the required user interactions, validation states, loading states, and error handling so the interface supports these behaviors: Register with phone number or email; Set delivery address with map pin; Save multiple delivery addresses.
acceptance_criteria: Frontend implements the required user registration user interactions.
Validation, loading, and error states for user registration are handled in the UI.
Frontend behavior for user registration is integrated correctly with backend APIs.

FR-01.title: Backend Implementation of User Registration
FR-01.description: Implement the backend logic for user registration. Build the required APIs, business rules, validation, permissions, persistence, and processing needed to support these behaviors: Register with phone number or email; Set delivery address with map pin; Save multiple delivery addresses.
acceptance_criteria: Backend logic for user registration is implemented and testable.
APIs and business rules for user registration enforce validation and security requirements.
Data handling for user registration is reliable and supports the required workflow.

FR-01.title: Integrate User Registration with External Services
FR-01.description: Integrate user registration with the external services or providers referenced by the SRS. Implement secure service connections, payload validation, error handling, and recovery behavior for any third-party dependency involved in the workflow.
acceptance_criteria: External or cross-system integrations required by user registration exchange the expected payloads successfully.
Retry, timeout, and failure handling for user registration integrations are implemented and observable.
Credentials, tokens, and outbound calls used by user registration are configured and secured correctly.

FR-01.title: Testing User Registration Functionality
FR-01.description: Create and execute tests for user registration. Cover the main success flows, failure scenarios, permission checks, validation rules, integrations, and operational edge cases described in the SRS so the implementation is safe to release.
acceptance_criteria: Tests cover the main success and failure scenarios for user registration.
Security, validation, and permission checks for user registration are verified.
Regression coverage exists for the most important user registration behaviors.
